features.evidence: i picked up an m3 macbook air at the start of april 2024 shortly after it was announced so i ve now been using it for around 6 months in this video i want to share my experience with this laptop for general use and as a content creator this is probably one of my most useful and favorite tech products that i ve ever used so let s get into it let s start with a bit of context before i got the macbook air i had been using a work owned m1 pro macbook pro to create my youtube videos however i always knew i wanted a device of my own and i had a few key criteria i needed from it firstly i wanted this stick with apple silicon because i had really enjoyed my experience with the macbook pro in terms of the power and efficiency the new device also had to be powerful enough to let me edit my videos on the go as well as at home i also needed it to be portable enough for me to take it anywhere without being too noticeably heavy in a backpack and a long battery life was essential on paper the new m3 macbook air was the obvious choice so when i noticed the 16 gb of ram and 52 gb of storage option in the starlight color for 100 off on amazon it seemed the ideal time to pick one up when i first got the air my immediate reaction was to compare it to the m1 pro macbook pro as i d been using that laptop for about 18 months at that point and mainly loving the experience there were a few things i quickly noticed about the air firstly the 13
value: battery and power: ['Up to 16 hours video streaming', 'Up to 11 hours wireless web', 'Built-in 36.5-watt-hour lithium-ion battery', '20W USB-C Power Adapter', 'USB-C Charge Cable (1.5 m)']

features.evidence: i picked up an m3 macbook air at the start of april 2024 shortly after it was announced so i ve now been using it for around 6 months in this video i want to share my experience with this laptop for general use and as a content creator this is probably one of my most useful and favorite tech products that i ve ever used so let s get into it let s start with a bit of context before i got the macbook air i had been using a work owned m1 pro macbook pro to create my youtube videos however i always knew i wanted a device of my own and i had a few key criteria i needed from it firstly i wanted this stick with apple silicon because i had really enjoyed my experience with the macbook pro in terms of the power and efficiency the new device also had to be powerful enough to let me edit my videos on the go as well as at home i also needed it to be portable enough for me to take it anywhere without being too noticeably heavy in a backpack and a long battery life was essential on paper the new m3 macbook air was the obvious choice so when i noticed the 16 gb of ram and 52 gb of storage option in the starlight color for 100 off on amazon it seemed the ideal time to pick one up when i first got the air my immediate reaction was to compare it to the m1 pro macbook pro as i d been using that laptop for about 18 months at that point and mainly loving the experience there were a few things i quickly noticed about the air firstly the 13

7 so around 41 lighter and this is extremely noticeable when you re carrying it in a bag as it doesn t feel it adds a lot of weight weight and it doesn t take up nearly as much space with it being substantially thinner and smaller than the pro this encourages me to take it on shorter day trips where i wouldn t have even considered taking the pro simply because it doesn t add much weight this means i can get a quick hour of editing or other youtube related work done wherever i am alongside this is the fact i can use this laptop with two external displays which was curiously absent as a feature on the m1 and m2 airs in my office i have a dual monitor setup which comes in very useful when scripting researching and photo and video editing the fact i can use the m3 air with both my displays was an essential feature for me one thing to note here though is that it s somewhat kneecapped as you can only do this when the air s lid is closed meaning you can t use the trap pad or keyboard when using two external displays however i use my logitech mx keys and mx master 3 mouse when i connect up to my monitors which makes this a complete non issue for me aside from the portability i also really the design of the m3 air which remains unchanged from the m2 a i particularly the relatively thin bezels around the screen and the uni war thickness of the body the notch doesn t seem to bother me at all and i found that i tend to forget it s even there i went for the starlight version as i thought it would be better for hiding fingerprints since some of the other colors and this seems to be true with the exception of the glossy apple logo i also just quite this color it feels halfway between gold and silver and it looks different depending on the lighting in benchmarks the macbook air performs better than the m1 pro macbook pro in single core performance but is definitely weaker in multi core and gpu performance due to it having four versus the eight cpu cores of the n1 pro mbook pro and 10 gpu cores as opposed to the 16 of the pro this was to be expected but what really matters is how it performs in real world use the majority of people using a macbook air will likely be using it for fairly light tasks which happened very quickly on this laptop it s super quick at opening apps and works really well for tasks web browsing dealing with emails scripting for youtube and watching video content where i was surprised though is how well it performs in more cpu and gpu heavy tasks which is what i was really interested in is probably about 80 of the tasks i do on this laptop involve photo or video editing i ve not experienced any real slowdown or difference in using this in lightroom even when doing heavier tasks ai noise reduction the same goes for video editing in dci resolve for the most part editing on the timeline is smooth as butter even with 4k 60fps 10bit footage the only place in resolve where i see a bit of a difference is in fusion which is the visual effects editor within resolve when i add a variety of effects to a clip it can slow down a bit but it s not an issue as long as you turn on the smart render cache setting for the timeline which pre renders clips so they play back smoothly the fact you re getting this performance from an ultra portable laptop without a fan is incredible compared to the performance we would get even a few years ago for me this makes the macbook air the perfect on the go editing machine in terms of heat dissipation the only real time i feel the laptop getting hot is when i m rendering out a video and resolve when i m doing this the body does get hot to the touch but the renders are still very fast for such a small fanless laptop you would obviously get more sustained performance out of the air if it had a fan but you d likely have to sacrifice some of the amazing portability to achieve that plus i ve never found myself really wanting for more power perhaps apart from when i m doing something fairly heavy in fusion another thing i ve not found myself wanting more of is battery life the battery on the air is truly excellent the air will quite easily last a few days if you re doing basic tasks browsing the internet answering emails or streaming video when i m doing heavier tasks photo or video editing the battery life on the m3 air does tend to run down substantially quicker but still lasts long enough for an extended editing session for most of the day which is ridiculously useful if you re traveling on a long plane journey for example finally let s talk about price the air that i have retails for 1,500 but i managed to pick it up for,400 and i ve seen it on sale since then for 1,350 however this version of the air can now be found refurbished from apple directly for 1,269 which i think is a cracking deal refurbished mac from apple are for all intents and purposes brand new laptops as they have a new battery fitted and apple fix anything that isn t working properly so that may be a better option for you if you re keen to buy one of these on the face of it this may seem a lot of money however when i take a step back and realize the value i ve got from my air in the last 6 months alone i d say it s 100 worth it it s enabled me to create videos from start to finish and it powers through more basic tasks super easily plus it can do all this anywhere because it doesn t sacrifice power to achieve its excellent portability so fundamentally the m3 macbook air after 6 months has been one of my favorite products i ve ever used the portability tied with the powerful m3 chip and great battery life make it a joy to use when i m creating content this i think if you re thinking about getting one of these for fairly basic tasks such as web browsing streaming content responding to emails then the base model will likely be enough however if you re a medium to heavy task user me then i d recommend going for the 16 gb of ram and 512 gb of storage model because it provides that extra bit of future proofing and headroom for multitasking but what do you think would you buy one of these let me know down in the comments below if you re interested in picking up the air then i ve linked it new in the description below but i d honestly encourage you to wait until it s available refurbished directly from apple as you ll get a brand new product for less money and if you already own one of these then i definitely recommend picking up the mini saura doc via the link in the description as it makes the working from home experience so much better otherwise as always it s been an absolute pleasure and i ll catch you in the next video cheers it s

6 in 60 hz ips display couldn t match the contrast smoothness and general quality of the 16 in 120 hz mini led display on the macbook pro bear in mind this was comparing the two laptops side by side however if you re not comparing the air alongside a macbook pro then the screen is honestly excellent on this laptop it provides accurate colors is super sharp and gets bright enough for 90 of the situations i find myself in i found that i ve not missed the high refresh rate of the pro because the majority of things i do on the laptop don t take advantage of this the only time you ll notice a difference is in scrolling and sometimes in the smoothness of animations but even with these actions i rarely consciously notice any issues with the smoothness for color dependent tasks such as video and photo editing the display is great although it s obviously a substantially smaller screen which can sometimes feel a bit cramped when compared to the 16 in pro especially in software such as da vinci resolve or lightroom watching content on the air is definitely not the same experience as when you re using the pro but that s mainly due to the class leading speaker system on the 16 in pro rather than the screens and that brings us on to the second difference i noticed between the two laptops there is a clear and obvious difference in the quality of the sound you get from both of these laptops with the pro the sound is full and catches all the nuanced low end in audio content this is not saying that the macbook air speakers are bad in fact i personally think they re very good considering the size of the body and the fact the audio is effectively bounced off the bottom of the screen but the macbook pro speakers are easily the best i ve ever heard in a laptop overall though if you weren t comparing the macbook air speakers to the exceptional pro speakers then they get loud enough without distorting and provide good clear audio for the class of device which is all i and probably most of you really need thirdly there was a clear difference between the port selection on both these laptops with the macbook pro having a good variety of ports whereas the macbook air only has two usbc ports plus a 3
value: display: ['Liquid Retina display', '13.0-inch (diagonal) LED-backlit display with IPS technology; 2408-by-1506 native resolution at 219 pixels per inch', '500 nits brightness', 'Support for 1 billion colors', 'sRGB color']

external display support: ['Supports one external display with up to a native resolution of 4K at 60Hz', 'Simultaneously supports the built-in display at full native resolution', 'USB 3 (USB-C) port supports native DisplayPort 1.4 speeds (up to HBR3)']

i picked up an m3 macbook air at the start of april 2024 shortly after it was announced so i ve now been using it for around 6 months in this video i want to share my experience with this laptop for general use and as a content creator this is probably one of my most useful and favorite tech products that i ve ever used so let s get into it let s start with a bit of context before i got the macbook air i had been using a work owned m1 pro macbook pro to create my youtube videos however i always knew i wanted a device of my own and i had a few key criteria i needed from it firstly i wanted this stick with apple silicon because i had really enjoyed my experience with the macbook pro in terms of the power and efficiency the new device also had to be powerful enough to let me edit my videos on the go as well as at home i also needed it to be portable enough for me to take it anywhere without being too noticeably heavy in a backpack and a long battery life was essential on paper the new m3 macbook air was the obvious choice so when i noticed the 16 gb of ram and 52 gb of storage option in the starlight color for 100 off on amazon it seemed the ideal time to pick one up when i first got the air my immediate reaction was to compare it to the m1 pro macbook pro as i d been using that laptop for about 18 months at that point and mainly loving the experience there were a few things i quickly noticed about the air firstly the 13

6 in 60 hz ips display couldn t match the contrast smoothness and general quality of the 16 in 120 hz mini led display on the macbook pro bear in mind this was comparing the two laptops side by side however if you re not comparing the air alongside a macbook pro then the screen is honestly excellent on this laptop it provides accurate colors is super sharp and gets bright enough for 90 of the situations i find myself in i found that i ve not missed the high refresh rate of the pro because the majority of things i do on the laptop don t take advantage of this the only time you ll notice a difference is in scrolling and sometimes in the smoothness of animations but even with these actions i rarely consciously notice any issues with the smoothness for color dependent tasks such as video and photo editing the display is great although it s obviously a substantially smaller screen which can sometimes feel a bit cramped when compared to the 16 in pro especially in software such as da vinci resolve or lightroom watching content on the air is definitely not the same experience as when you re using the pro but that s mainly due to the class leading speaker system on the 16 in pro rather than the screens and that brings us on to the second difference i noticed between the two laptops there is a clear and obvious difference in the quality of the sound you get from both of these laptops with the pro the sound is full and catches all the nuanced low end in audio content this is not saying that the macbook air speakers are bad in fact i personally think they re very good considering the size of the body and the fact the audio is effectively bounced off the bottom of the screen but the macbook pro speakers are easily the best i ve ever heard in a laptop overall though if you weren t comparing the macbook air speakers to the exceptional pro speakers then they get loud enough without distorting and provide good clear audio for the class of device which is all i and probably most of you really need thirdly there was a clear difference between the port selection on both these laptops with the macbook pro having a good variety of ports whereas the macbook air only has two usbc ports plus a 3

review_summary: i picked up an m3 macbook air at the start of april 2024 shortly after it was announced so i ve now been using it for around 6 months in this video i want to share my experience with this laptop for general use and as a content creator this is probably one of my most useful and favorite tech products that i ve ever used so let s get into it let s start with a bit of context before i got the macbook air i had been using a work owned m1 pro macbook pro to create my youtube videos however i always knew i wanted a device of my own and i had a few key criteria i needed from it firstly i wanted this stick with apple silicon because i had really enjoyed my experience with the macbook pro in terms of the power and efficiency the new device also had to be powerful enough to let me edit my videos on the go as well as at home i also needed it to be portable enough for me to take it anywhere without being too noticeably heavy in a backpack and a long battery life was essential on paper the new m3 macbook air was the obvious choice so when i noticed the 16 gb of ram and 52 gb of storage option in the starlight color for 100 off on amazon it seemed the ideal time to pick one up when i first got the air my immediate reaction was to compare it to the m1 pro macbook pro as i d been using that laptop for about 18 months at that point and mainly loving the experience there were a few things i quickly noticed about the air firstly the 13 i picked up an m3 macbook air at the start of april 2024 shortly after it was announced so i ve now been using it for around 6 months in this video i want to share my experience with this laptop for general use and as a content creator this is probably one of my most useful and favorite tech products that i ve ever used so let s get into it let s start with a bit of context before i got the macbook air i had been using a work owned m1 pro macbook pro to create my youtube videos however i always knew i wanted a device of my own and i had a few key criteria i needed from it firstly i wanted this stick with apple silicon because i had really enjoyed my experience with the macbook pro in terms of the power and efficiency the new device also had to be powerful enough to let me edit my videos on the go as well as at home i also needed it to be portable enough for me to take it anywhere without being too noticeably heavy in a backpack and a long battery life was essential on paper the new m3 macbook air was the obvious choice so when i noticed the 16 gb of ram and 52 gb of storage option in the starlight color for 100 off on amazon it seemed the ideal time to pick one up when i first got the air my immediate reaction was to compare it to the m1 pro macbook pro as i d been using that laptop for about 18 months at that point and mainly loving the experience there were a few things i quickly noticed about the air firstly the 13 7 so around 41 lighter and this is extremely noticeable when you re carrying it in a bag as it doesn t feel it adds a lot of weight weight and it doesn t take up nearly as much space with it being substantially thinner and smaller than the pro this encourages me to take it on shorter day trips where i wouldn t have even considered taking the pro simply because it doesn t add much weight this means i can get a quick hour of editing or other youtube related work done wherever i am alongside this is the fact i can use this laptop with two external displays which was curiously absent as a feature on the m1 and m2 airs in my office i have a dual monitor setup which comes in very useful when scripting researching and photo and video editing the fact i can use the m3 air with both my displays was an essential feature for me one thing to note here though is that it s somewhat kneecapped as you can only do this when the air s lid is closed meaning you can t use the trap pad or keyboard when using two external displays however i use my logitech mx keys and mx master 3 mouse when i connect up to my monitors which makes this a complete non issue for me aside from the portability i also really the design of the m3 air which remains unchanged from the m2 a i particularly the relatively thin bezels around the screen and the uni war thickness of the body the notch doesn t seem to bother me at all and i found that i tend to forget it s even there i went for the starlight version as i thought it would be better for hiding fingerprints since some of the other colors and this seems to be true with the exception of the glossy apple logo i also just quite this color it feels halfway between gold and silver and it looks different depending on the lighting in benchmarks the macbook air performs better than the m1 pro macbook pro in single core performance but is definitely weaker in multi core and gpu performance due to it having four versus the eight cpu cores of the n1 pro mbook pro and 10 gpu cores as opposed to the 16 of the pro this was to be expected but what really matters is how it performs in real world use the majority of people using a macbook air will likely be using it for fairly light tasks which happened very quickly on this laptop it s super quick at opening apps and works really well for tasks web browsing dealing with emails scripting for youtube and watching video content where i was surprised though is how well it performs in more cpu and gpu heavy tasks which is what i was really interested in is probably about 80 of the tasks i do on this laptop involve photo or video editing i ve not experienced any real slowdown or difference in using this in lightroom even when doing heavier tasks ai noise reduction the same goes for video editing in dci resolve for the most part editing on the timeline is smooth as butter even with 4k 60fps 10bit footage the only place in resolve where i see a bit of a difference is in fusion which is the visual effects editor within resolve when i add a variety of effects to a clip it can slow down a bit but it s not an issue as long as you turn on the smart render cache setting for the timeline which pre renders clips so they play back smoothly the fact you re getting this performance from an ultra portable laptop without a fan is incredible compared to the performance we would get even a few years ago for me this makes the macbook air the perfect on the go editing machine in terms of heat dissipation the only real time i feel the laptop getting hot is when i m rendering out a video and resolve when i m doing this the body does get hot to the touch but the renders are still very fast for such a small fanless laptop you would obviously get more sustained performance out of the air if it had a fan but you d likely have to sacrifice some of the amazing portability to achieve that plus i ve never found myself really wanting for more power perhaps apart from when i m doing something fairly heavy in fusion another thing i ve not found myself wanting more of is battery life the battery on the air is truly excellent the air will quite easily last a few days if you re doing basic tasks browsing the internet answering emails or streaming video when i m doing heavier tasks photo or video editing the battery life on the m3 air does tend to run down substantially quicker but still lasts long enough for an extended editing session for most of the day which is ridiculously useful if you re traveling on a long plane journey for example finally let s talk about price the air that i have retails for 1,500 but i managed to pick it up for,400 and i ve seen it on sale since then for 1,350 however this version of the air can now be found refurbished from apple directly for 1,269 which i think is a cracking deal refurbished mac from apple are for all intents and purposes brand new laptops as they have a new battery fitted and apple fix anything that isn t working properly so that may be a better option for you if you re keen to buy one of these on the face of it this may seem a lot of money however when i take a step back and realize the value i ve got from my air in the last 6 months alone i d say it s 100 worth it it s enabled me to create videos from start to finish and it powers through more basic tasks super easily plus it can do all this anywhere because it doesn t sacrifice power to achieve its excellent portability so fundamentally the m3 macbook air after 6 months has been one of my favorite products i ve ever used the portability tied with the powerful m3 chip and great battery life make it a joy to use when i m creating content this i think if you re thinking about getting one of these for fairly basic tasks such as web browsing streaming content responding to emails then the base model will likely be enough however if you re a medium to heavy task user me then i d recommend going for the 16 gb of ram and 512 gb of storage model because it provides that extra bit of future proofing and headroom for multitasking but what do you think would you buy one of these let me know down in the comments below if you re interested in picking up the air then i ve linked it new in the description below but i d honestly encourage you to wait until it s available refurbished directly from apple as you ll get a brand new product for less money and if you already own one of these then i definitely recommend picking up the mini saura doc via the link in the description as it makes the working from home experience so much better otherwise as always it s been an absolute pleasure and i ll catch you in the next video cheers it s
